title: Tinker Mode 0: pre-flight scan for assigned-to-tinker work routed via expansion-tracker (assigned_to='tinker') and open-issues (status='assigned-tinker'), mirroring the existing assigned-analyst reader pattern. Closes the structural gap where routing fields written by analyst/decider/integrity for tinker have no automated consumer and items sit indefinitely (ISS-1285 34d+, ISS-2134 6d, EXP-425 48h before operator-direct rescue).
author: tinker
source_finding: DIRECTIVE-20260525-003 (tinker work-surfacing design). Tinker dispatcher consumes (1) operator-directives/*.json status='pending' and (2) mtime-staleness check + queue-history metrics. It DOES NOT iterate assigned_to/status routing fields. Three real items currently invisible to tinker: ISS-1285 (34d, infra, minor — raw-text staleness, OBE per EXP-306), ISS-2134 (6d, operational, major — WIN-070 re-push loop already root-caused by PROP-048), EXP-425 (48h, integrity_finding — operator-direct rescued 2026-05-25 via orphan-backfill).
summary: Add a pre-flight 'Mode 0: Assigned-Tinker Scan' to monitor/prompts/tinker.md, placed AFTER the existing operator-directive discovery step and BEFORE the Mode 1–4 trigger evaluation. The scan reads both routing fields (open-issues.json status='assigned-tinker' and expansion-tracker.json assigned_to='tinker' AND status NOT in {complete, integrated, archived}). If items found, elevate the top item (severity-then-age) to the run's primary task and route via class_hint to Mode 1 (mechanical close) or Mode 4 (PROP authoring). Per-run cap of 1 item maintains the existing 'one mode, deep work' discipline. Closure path: tinker writes HNOTE action='close_iss_batch' to decider after action ships, decider closes the ISS on its next run.
problem:
Three agents (analyst, decider, integrity) write routing-to-tinker fields with the implicit expectation that tinker picks the items up. But tinker has no reader for these fields. The asymmetry is most visible against the parallel `assigned-analyst` path, which has TWO working readers (analyst.md line 168 dispatcher filter + analyst-baby.md line 115 PROP-029 orphan safety net). Items routed to tinker sit invisibly:

  - ISS-1285 (raw-text/02-wins.txt staleness): routed 2026-05-11, sat 14+ days even though the routing_reason itself notes it as OBE.
  - ISS-2134 (WIN-070 re-push loop): routed 2026-05-19, root cause fixed by PROP-048 same day, but ISS sits because no tinker scan closes it.
  - EXP-425 (expansion-tracker gap audit): routed 2026-05-18, sat 48h until 2026-05-25 operator-direct rescue.

The defect class is `routing field written, no consumer wired up`. Same shape as the decider's action-typed HNOTE handler losing narrative HNOTEs (DIRECTIVE-20260525-003 cross-reference) — different agent, same bug.

alternatives_considered.name: Reader-side Mode 0 scan (mirrors assigned-analyst)
alternatives_considered.summary: Tinker pre-flight scans open-issues + expansion-tracker for routing fields, picks top item, routes to Mode 1 or Mode 4 based on class_hint.
pros: Single prompt-file edit (tinker.md). Blast radius minimal.
Mirrors a known-working pattern (analyst's assigned-analyst reader).
No coordination with other agents required (writer-side prompts unchanged).
Operator's at-a-glance routing view in open-issues.json is preserved.
Items get picked up the same run they're written, not after a timeout.
cons: Tinker accumulates a second inbox alongside operator-directives. Mitigated by per-run cap of 1 + deterministic priority ordering.

Risk that an operator-judgment-only item (e.g., re-enable sloppytoppy) gets a premature PROP. Mitigated by Mode 0 sub-routing: if class is genuinely opaque, tinker writes operator-attention line to latest-tinker-summary.txt and leaves the item open.

alternatives_considered.name: Writer-side ban: forbid assigned-tinker fields, require directive writes
alternatives_considered.summary: Update analyst.md, decider.md, structure-integrity.md so 'routing to tinker' means writing monitor/tinker/operator-directives/DIRECTIVE-AUTO-NNN.json with status='pending'. Integrity scan flags any leftover routing-field usage as a defect.
cons: Three writer prompts to update + ongoing coordination. Each future agent that wants to route work to tinker must learn the directive pattern.
Loses parity with assigned-analyst, which is field-based and works fine. Inconsistent two-protocol design.
Loses the operator's at-a-glance routing visibility in open-issues.json (an ISS routed to tinker would no longer appear as assigned-tinker; only the directive would exist, in a separate directory).
Higher operator-load — operator currently uses status='assigned-tinker' as a triage signal when scanning open-issues; would have to also scan operator-directives/ for AUTO-prefixed entries.
Migrating existing assigned-tinker items requires a one-time conversion script + careful timing.

alternatives_considered.name: Graveyard sweep: auto-promote stale assigned-tinker items to directives after N days
alternatives_considered.summary: Tinker pre-flight scans for assigned-to-tinker items older than 7d and auto-creates DIRECTIVE-AUTO-<ISS-ID>.json with status='pending'. Items aged <7d sit untouched.
pros: Items can never be lost — guaranteed surfacing after 7d.
Preserves both routing-field visibility AND directive-driven processing.
cons: 7-day latency for normal items even when the queue is empty.
Directive proliferation: every routed item eventually becomes a directive file. Operator-directives/ directory grows.
Doubles bookkeeping (ISS + directive for the same target). Risk of divergence if one is updated but not the other.
Doesn't solve the immediate-surfacing problem — only the never-lost problem.

alternatives_considered.name: Hybrid: option_a immediate scan + option_c graveyard fallback when cap exceeded
alternatives_considered.summary: Mode 0 picks up items immediately. If cap-overflow for N consecutive runs, oldest items auto-promote to directives.
pros: Best-of-both: immediate handling AND never-lost guarantee.
Layered: directives only created when reader-path can't keep up.
cons: More complexity, two moving parts to maintain.
Premature optimization for a queue that currently has 2 items. The cap-overflow scenario hasn't actually happened.
Can be layered onto option_a later via a tiny script if backlog growth makes it necessary.

recommendation_rationale: Option (a) is the smallest, most consistent change that solves the immediate problem. It mirrors the analyst's working reader pattern, requires no writer-side coordination, preserves operator visibility, and keeps tinker's prompt structure intact. The only real concern (queue grows unboundedly) is addressed by the per-run cap, and is empirically not a problem today (queue depth = 2 items). If the cap-overflow case ever materializes, option_d can be layered on top via a script — but building it now is premature.

counter_considerations.concern: Tinker now has TWO inboxes (operator-directives + assigned-to-tinker scan). Could confuse priority.
counter_considerations.resolution: Priority order is fixed and documented: (1) pending operator-directives first (operator's explicit asks), (2) assigned-to-tinker scan items second (agent-routed work). If both have items, tinker processes the directive that run and the scan items next run. Per-run cap of 1 prevents inbox blending.

counter_considerations.concern: Closure responsibility ambiguity — if tinker actions an ISS, who closes it?
counter_considerations.resolution: Tinker writes monitor/decisions/human-notes.json HNOTE with action='close_iss_batch' and iss_ids=[<id>] after action ships. Decider's existing HNOTE handler (close_iss_batch verb is already wired up per CLAUDE.md) closes the ISS on its next run. Same pattern the operator already uses today.

counter_considerations.concern: Mode 0 might force tinker to author a PROP when the item is operator-judgment only.
counter_considerations.resolution: Mode 0 dispatch is class-hint-aware. ISS records carry a class_hint field; if present and == 'verification' → Mode 1 mechanical close. If class is opaque or operator-judgment is clearly needed (e.g., 'should we re-enable sloppytoppy'), tinker writes an operator-attention summary line to latest-tinker-summary.txt and leaves the ISS open (no false PROP). This is an explicit Mode 0 outcome: 'operator-decision-needed, escalated to summary'.

counter_considerations.concern: What if both a directive AND an assigned-tinker ISS exist for the same target (ISS-2134 + DIRECTIVE-20260519-002)?
counter_considerations.resolution: Directive wins (richer context, operator-filed). Mode 0 scan skips ISSs whose evidence cites an existing operator-directive (by ID lookup or topic match). Tinker processes via the directive; when directive completes, write HNOTE to close the paired ISS.

counter_considerations.concern: Already-resolved items still in open-issues.json (e.g., ISS-2134 already fixed by PROP-048) would be picked up as work.
counter_considerations.resolution: Feature, not bug. Tinker verifies state (PROP-048 integrated, zero re-push events since), then writes HNOTE close_iss_batch with fixed_by='PROP-048' rationale. This is the 'OBE close' path. Backlog drains via this path.

counter_considerations.concern: If tinker mis-classifies an item, no curmudgeon-equivalent review.
counter_considerations.resolution: Mode 0 actions emit a finding into the run report with category='mode-0-action'. Findings are auditable. If a Mode 0 action turns out wrong, operator can file a directive reversing it. Same accountability pattern as Mode 1–4 mechanical fixes.

implementation_steps.change: After the 'Pre-flight: Operator Directive Discovery' section and BEFORE 'Pre-flight: Backlog-Trend Computation', insert a new section 'Pre-flight: Mode 0 — Assigned-To-Tinker Scan' with the bash + node block (see implementation_pseudocode).

Add 'Mode 0' definition to the Dispatcher section between operator-directives and Mode 1, with priority order: (i) pending operator-directive — preempts everything, (ii) Mode 0 scan items — preempts Mode 1–4, (iii) Mode 1–4 — fall through if neither has work.

Document the Mode 0 closure path: 'After actioning an assigned-tinker ISS, write HNOTE to monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with action="close_iss_batch", iss_ids=[<id>], note_text=<your closure reasoning>. Decider closes on next run. Dual-write the HNOTE to FUSE + clone per CLAUDE.md Human Notes Rule.'

Document the four valid Mode 0 outcomes per item: (a) close-as-OBE via HNOTE (item already resolved by other work), (b) author PROP via Mode 4 (structural fix needed), (c) escalate-to-operator via latest-tinker-summary.txt + leave ISS open (operator-judgment-needed), (d) defer via report finding (work needed but blocked; ISS stays open with note).

edge_cases.case: Item exists in BOTH open-issues (status='assigned-tinker') AND has a paired operator-directive (e.g., DIRECTIVE-20260519-002 + ISS-2134).
edge_cases.handling: Directive wins. Mode 0 skips ISSs whose `assigned_tinker_reason` or `notes` field cites an existing pending directive ID (string-match on DIRECTIVE-NNN). After directive completes, the directive's completion handler writes the close_iss_batch HNOTE for the paired ISS.

edge_cases.case: Item already resolved by upstream work (e.g., ISS-2134 fixed by PROP-048 days before tinker sees it).
edge_cases.handling: Verify upstream resolution (load the cited PROP, check status=integrated, check evidence of zero recurrence). If verified, action = HNOTE close_iss_batch with fixed_by='<PROP-id>' and rationale citing verification evidence. Same shape as decider's M1-wontfix corrective ledger.

edge_cases.case: Item has assigned_to='tinker' AND status='complete' (EXP-425 today).
edge_cases.handling: Excluded by the status NOT IN {complete,integrated,archived} filter. Tinker no longer surfaces these. If a completed item somehow re-surfaces, treat as a noop and log a finding for investigation.

edge_cases.case: Operator files a directive AND a routing field hits Mode 0 in the same run.
edge_cases.handling: Operator-directive preempts. Per-run cap of 1 means scan items wait. Note in report: 'mode_0_deferred_to_next_run: <item-id>'.

Per PROP-030 backlog-trend pattern: emit a finding with category='mode-0-backlog' severity=moderate when item count >5, severity=major when >10. Operator escalation triggers if >20. (Not implementing graveyard auto-promote in this PROP — option_d layered separately if needed.)

edge_cases.case: Tinker action turns out wrong (e.g., closed an ISS that shouldn't have been).
edge_cases.handling: Mode 0 actions emit a finding with category='mode-0-action' and full evidence. Operator can revert via direct HNOTE re-opening the ISS, or file a corrective directive. No automated rollback (same as decider M1 corrective-ledger pattern).
Tinker's pre-flight always reads from the clone (which workspace-sync rescues every hour). Pre-flight runs after the clone is fresh from PRELUDE_AUTH-verified clone. No FUSE read in Mode 0.

idempotency_contract: Mode 0 is read-mostly; the only writes are (1) HNOTE creation when closing an ISS — idempotent because HNOTEs are uniquely identified by note_id + the closure path checks the ISS isn't already closed before writing; (2) operator-attention summary line in latest-tinker-summary.txt — overwritten every tinker run anyway, not idempotency-sensitive; (3) PROP file creation when routing to Mode 4 — same idempotency as today's PROP authoring. If a single ISS is picked up by Mode 0 in two consecutive runs (because the close HNOTE hasn't been consumed yet), the second run sees the closure HNOTE in human-notes.json status='pending' and skips re-actioning the same ISS.
verification_pattern:
After PROP-060 ships:
  1. Within 24h, ISS-1285 and ISS-2134 should both be closed-as-OBE (tinker writes HNOTE, decider closes).
  2. Within 7 days, queue of assigned-to-tinker items should drain to <=1 steady-state.
  3. tinker run reports should contain a `mode_0_scan` section showing items considered + top-pick + action taken.
  4. No new EXP-425-style 48h+ sittings of routed items.

Rollback signal: if Mode 0 emits >2 'mode-0-action-reverted' findings in a 7-day window (operator reversing tinker actions), revisit the dispatch logic. Likely fix: tighter class_hint requirements before Mode 4 dispatch.
blast_radius: Tinker prompt only. No data file schema changes. No other agent prompt edits. workspace-sync.md unchanged (no new file ownership). build.js unchanged. CLAUDE.md gets a one-line File Ownership table note: 'Tinker also reads assigned_to=tinker and status=assigned-tinker from these files (per PROP-060 Mode 0).'
operator_action_required: Operator review of recommended option (option_a). If approved, tinker can self-apply per the existing self-fix discipline (modifying its own prompt is within scope per tinker.md's 'Conservative self-fixes' rule — though prompt edits to one's own dispatcher are at the upper edge of that scope, so HNOTE-first then operator-greenlight is the safer flow).
rejection_criteria: Reject if: (a) operator prefers a different option from the four listed, or (b) operator wants the writer-side ban (option_b) to enforce a single inbox, or (c) operator wants tinker to remain pull-only and prefers the directive-creation-by-other-agents path. In any rejection case, the directive-aligned drain audit in Step 3 of DIRECTIVE-20260525-003 (this run's findings) still stands and the two open assigned-tinker items can be closed manually.
related_props: PROP-029 (analyst orphan safety-net — pattern to mirror)
PROP-030 (backlog-trend metrics — Mode 0 hooks into this)
PROP-048 (decider win-new dedup — resolved ISS-2134 root cause)
PROP-026 (burndown — drained open-issues backlog; queue now small enough for Mode 0 to keep pace)
estimated_implementation_effort: 30-45 min Opus tinker run for self-apply (prompt edit ~80 lines, verification test by running Mode 0 against current ISS-1285 and ISS-2134). Or operator-direct cowork-session if operator wants to review the prompt edit before commit.
